title: Deep-Fry Immersion Heating — Oil Temperature Control
content: Deep frying requires oil temperature 160–190°C. Immersion heating element power P = V²/R maintains temperature via PID controller with thermocouple feedback. Maillard reaction and starch gelatinisation occur at 140–165°C.
extended_content: Potato fries: blanch at 150°C for 3–4 min (first fry, sets structure), finish at 175–185°C for 2–3 min (crisping). Oil volume/food ratio > 6:1 prevents temperature crash. Heat loss to food: Q = m_food × c_p × ΔT (c_p water ≈ 4200 J/kg·K). Temperature recovery time < 2 min with 2 kW heater for 2 L oil. Dual-sided fry basket: mesh basket that inverts halfway through cycle via servo/motor, ensuring uniform browning. Safety: thermal cutout at 230°C, earthed heating element, IP splash-proof enclosure. Oil degradation: smoke point sunflower oil 227°C, palm oil 235°C.
formula: P = V²/R ;  Q = m·c_p·ΔT ;  t_recovery ≈ m_oil·c_oil·ΔT/P
units: P: W, T: °C, Q: J, m: kg, c_p: J/kg·K
source: Fellows, Food Processing Technology, 4th ed.; Blumenthal, The Science of Frying, Food Technology 1991; ASHRAE HVAC Applications
